steps: Translate equivalent ratios into a proportion: "5 to x is equivalent to y to 8" means \(\frac{5}{x} = \frac{y}{8}\).
Cross-multiply: \(5 \cdot 8 = y \cdot x\), so \(40 = xy\). Divide both sides by \(y\): \(x = \frac{40}{y}\).

Evaluate each choice:
  • A: \(x = \frac{5y}{8}\) — this comes from incorrectly writing \(\frac{5}{y} = \frac{x}{8}\), reversing the second ratio. Incorrect.
  • B: \(x = \frac{8y}{5}\) — this would come from \(\frac{5}{8} = \frac{x}{y}\), mixing up the original ratios. Incorrect.
  • C: \(x = \frac{y}{40}\) — inverts the correct answer; would mean \(xy = 1\) only when \(x = 1/40\). Incorrect.
  • D: \(x = \frac{40}{y}\) — matches the result \(xy = 40\) derived by cross-multiplication. That matches choice D — D is correct.
